prompt: Wide editorial industrial photography, 16:9 aspect ratio. A Romanian stoneworker in a navy work apron is applying a clear impregnation treatment to a horizontal slab of polished beige travertine on a workshop bench, using a manual pressurized hand pump sprayer (vermorel) held in the right side of the frame, fine even mist landing on the dry stone surface. A folded grey microfiber cloth and a small 1-litre Delta Research SEAL tin (the actual product shown in the attached reference image) sit on the workshop bench in the mid-right area, exactly as they would appear in a real workshop — naturally placed, realistic lighting and shadows matching the rest of the scene, slight wear/dust as expected on working equipment. The left third of the frame shows the empty stone surface and an out-of-focus industrial workshop background with concrete floor and stacked stone slabs. Natural overcast daylight from a large workshop window, no harsh shadows, no dramatic lighting, neutral colour balance, realistic textures, professional B2B trade catalogue aesthetic, warm earth tones with terracotta accents. Photorealistic.
